ogTitle: Iwantja vs James Well: Climate Safety

heading: Iwantja vs James Well — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Iwantja, South Australia and James Well, South Australia. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Iwantja has a lower overall climate risk score (35/100) compared to James Well (35/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Iwantja or James Well safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Iwantja has a flood risk score of 43/100, while James Well scores 20/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Iwantja scores 58/100 for bushfire risk. James Well scores 50/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Iwantja or James Well?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Iwantja has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 0.8% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
